publicationstmt.publisher: Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid prepared according to Describing Archives: A Content Standard (DACS).

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

Finding aid updated to include accruals.

corpname.part: Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The papers are arranged in two series. I. Student work, 1972-1975. II. Graphic design collection, 1960-2015.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Lawrence Wolfson (born 1951) grew up on Long Island. He received a Bachelor of Fine Arts degree from the Rhode Island School of Design in 1973 and a Master of Fine Arts degree from Yale, in 1975.
Wolfson worked for several years as a Senior Designer at the New York design firm, Pentagram, before founding his own firm, Lawrence Wolfson Design, in 1986.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Lawrence Wolfson Papers (ALSC MS 1). Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections, Yale Library.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Papers include sketches and other manuscript material created by Lawrence Wolfson while a student in the Master of Fine Arts program in Graphic Design at Yale University between 1973 and 1975, as well as a final project artists' book created at RISD in 1972-1973 and submitted as part of the portfolio for his application to Yale. The represented Yale courses include drawing, typography, design, and studio photography. Among his instructors were Inge Druckrey, Norman Ives, Armin Hoffman, Herbert Matter, Samia Halaby, Paul Rand, and Alvin Eisenman.

Also included is Wolfson's collection of posters and other ephemera – representing the graphic design work of Armin Hofmann, Wolfgang Weingart, Ken Komai, Willi Kunz, Dan Friedman, and others. Soon after graduating from Yale, Wolfson travelled to the Kunstgewerbeschule in Basel, Switzerland to visit Armin Hofmann and meet Weingart. He ammassed the bulk of this collection of broadsides and other publications during this trip.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright is retained by the creator(s) of this collection for materials they have authored or otherwise produced. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized by place or course instructor

did.unittitle: Rhode Island School of Design course

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: RISD work used as part of the portfolio for the application to the Yale graphic design program.

did.unittitle: Book design of essay by Sergei Eisenstein
Type layout studies using a typewriter and photostat map, "A Guide to Direct Transportation to and from New Haven"
Object studies, pencil on paper; composition study, freehand painted composition, Plaka paint on paper, 4 pieces
Object study – b & w photograph in different lighting situations, printed on card, 3 pieces
Object studies - Plaka paint on paper, 6 pieces
Composition studies, Plaka paint on board – "Composition in five columns", "Composition in a grid"
Final graphic translation of the object, Plaka paint on board
8 black and white photographs (most 5 x 7") mounted on card
"Interior study," "Planes in space," pencil on paper. 5 pieces
Armin Hofmann color and typography course
Color family on different backgrounds, Plaka paint on board, 3 pieces 11 x 11"
Black and white and color family applied to Art/Architecture symbol, Plaka paint on board, 5 pieces
Sketches for symbol for the Art and Architecture Schools, pencil on paper.
Letterform design, Plaka paint on board, 3 pieces, 11 x 11"
Educational Concepts / School of Design, Basel, Switzerland
Thoughts on the study and making of visual signs
Posters: Documentation, edition of 50-90 each, Blatt 16, Blatt 17, Blatt 18, Blatt 19, Blatt 20
Posters: Documentation, edition of 50-90 each, Blatt 21, Blatt 6, Blatt 7, Blatt 9, Blatt 10
Posters: Documentation, edition of 50-90 each, Blatt 11, Blatt 12, Blatt 13, Blatt 14, Blatt 15
New year's card signed by WK, lecture postcard, gallery map
Ives-Silliman pamphlet, Kunstgewerbemuseum Zürich exhibition catalog
Posters: designed for Columbia University Graduate School of Architecture, Planning and Preservation, 8 pieces.
Posters: designed to advertise programs offered by Columbia University School of Architecture and Planning (3)
Posters: designed for photographer, Fredrich Cantor (3).
Posters: designed for Columbia University Graduate School of Architecture and Planning for lectures and exhibitions, 29 total
Yale School of Art brochure-poster designed by Inge Druckrey
The Basel School of Design and Its Philosophy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This portfolio lacks "The Declaration of the Rights of Man" by W. H. Allner, and "Epictetus" by Jean Varda.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This portfolio lacks "Goethe" by Antonio Frasconi; "Epictetus" by Leo Lionni; "Mr. Justice Holmes" by Jack Gregory; "Jane Addams" by George Giusti; "Benedict Spinoza" by Richard Lindner; "Adam Smith" by Arthur Williams; "William Graham Sumner" by Joseph Gering; "Plato" by Egbert Jacobson and Adrian Lozano.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This portfolio lacks Bhagavad-Gita" by Mohan B. Samant, and has an extra copy of "Horace Mann" by Lester Beall.
